title: [FIRRTL] LowerToHW breaks on shr of zero-width
body:
Input:
```
firrtl.circuit "ShrZW" {
  firrtl.module @ShrZW(in %x: !firrtl.uint<0>, out %out: !firrtl.uint<1>) attributes {convention = #firrtl<convention scalarized>} {
    %0 = firrtl.shr %x, 5 : (!firrtl.uint<0>) -> !firrtl.uint<1>
    firrtl.connect %out, %0 : !firrtl.uint<1>, !firrtl.uint<1>
  }
}
```

Running `--lower-firrtl-to-hw` on this:

```
shr_issue.mlir:3:10: error: 'firrtl.shr' op LowerToHW couldn't handle this operation
    %0 = firrtl.shr %x, 5 : (!firrtl.uint<0>) -> !firrtl.uint<1>
         ^
shr_issue.mlir:3:10: note: see current operation: %1 = "firrtl.shr"(%0) {amount = 5 : i32} : (!firrtl.uint<0>) -> !firrtl.uint<1>
```

LowerToHW should not require folders, especially re:eliding zero-width constructs.
